column5:
1. Visitor End:  
① Utilize the kindergarten renovation fence to increase site signage and guidance within the community to ensure smooth natural visitor traffic; ② Continuously strengthen the online communication of the clearance phase, conduct cross-visit follow-ups with customers who visited from March to June but did not make a purchase, to stimulate demand from existing customer resources; ③ Strengthen the communication of the clearance phase, collaborate with Ke.com and 58.com, and leverage the momentum of strong sales to drive further interest; ④ Utilize the clubhouse facilities, collaborate with property management and customer relations, and organize ongoing engagement activities for current and soon-to-be residents to promote the clearance phase and referral policies.  

2. Conversion End:  
① Based on the remaining inventory structure, develop individual guiding scripts and pricing discount strategies, provide one-on-one guidance and pressure tactics to recommend suitable units to targeted customers; ② After moving the reception location into the community, standardize the reception route, set fixed reception points within the community with corresponding scripts, enforce reception duration requirements, and increase on-the-spot purchases and revisit rates; ③ Enhance the details of post-sale property services, allow customers to experience the quality of future property services through their visit route, thereby improving conversion rates; ④ Maintain the fresh appearance of remaining ready-to-move-in units, produce maintenance videos for ready-to-move-in units to ensure a positive customer experience.  

3. Other Special Projects:  
① Focus on the latest reception location and route, continuously optimize sales tools and detailed scripts;  
② Combine with the June "Leisure Life Festival" theme, utilize the clubhouse real-life setting and the lifestyle of current residents to produce lifestyle summer night scene videos.

column3: Already purchased 1 unit, 1.44 million yuan, expected to complete 90 million yuan; already signed 100,000 yuan, expected to sign 8.624 million yuan.
column5:

Visitor End: ① Focus on the additional launch of Building 10, highlighting core values such as "the last building of the entire phase," "rare mid-rise building," and "upgraded standard finishes" to strengthen promotion and increase visitor traffic; ② Combine with the summer gift rights distribution event at the already launched sales office of Shanshan Outlets, and use part of these as visitor gifts for clients; ③ Continue to emphasize the clearance special-priced units in Building 4 and the additional launch of the last building in the entire phase for Building 10, and coordinate with distribution channels and in-house channels for live broadcast promotion; ④ Continuously produce construction progress comparison charts, release in advance the message that the building will be topped off by the end of the month as a near-completed property, combined with clearance special-priced units and the additional launch, to enhance online lead generation; ⑤ Preheat online for the "618 Home Buying Festival" event node.  
2. Conversion End: ① Package the group-purchase discount message for the "618 Home Buying Festival" to promote increased conversion during the week; ② Arrange property consultants and planners to visit units in Building 10, take photos and videos of the view from mid-to-high floor units, and use them as tools for consultants to encourage finalization of purchases; ③ Switch the parking space discount message for the "66 Urban Skyline Life Festival" and incorporate it into the "618" event, to promote conversion and purchase commitments.  
3. Other Special Projects: Collaborate with the engineering team to optimize the new model room layout and elevator experience; develop dual finish standard guidance scripts and conduct training.

column3: 已认购16套、11.74 million, expected to complete 24.74 million; signed 3.43 million, expected to sign 37.49 million.
column4:
① Customers have strong resistance to off-plan purchases, with heavy wait-and-see attitudes, making it difficult to invite returning customers for follow-up visits.  
② Competitor pricing (Wanjī has reduced prices to a starting price of 3,900 RMB per square meter) continues to decline, lowering customer price expectations.  
③ There are 47 remaining units in the second batch (only 12 units left out of 122 total, mostly in high-rise areas), which will enter a large-scale off-plan sales situation in 2026.
column5:
1. Visitor End: ① Focus on the additional launch of Building 17, highlight the advantage of upgraded unit sizes, and create a differentiated competition with the first and second batches of units. Leverage key moments such as the 618 event, mid-year sales push, and pre-occupancy inspections of the first batch units to collaborate with distributors and KOLs to attract customers. ② Utilize the "Sharing Home" policy to deeply engage employees, homeowners, competitors, and cross-industry resources to broaden customer sources. ③ Continuously expand group-buying opportunities from government agencies and enterprises. ④ Increase the frequency of homeowner events to enhance referral rates from existing customers.

2. Conversion End: ① Strengthen the promotion of the advantages of Building 17's unit designs and provide training on counterarguments regarding delivery timelines to minimize the negative perception of delivery delays. ② Tailor communication strategies for each unit in the first and second batches, clarify the sales logic for the third batch, and maximize customer conversion. ③ Package key moments: Use the 618 Housing Festival and the additional launch of Building 17 to create urgency and drive customer decisions.

3. Others: Finalize the finishing standards for the model unit of Building 19 as soon as possible and open it for external display.

33 units purchased for 2.26 million, expected to complete 10 million; 1.21 million signed, expected to sign 10 million.
column4:
1. Weak customer demand: Long-term continuous pressure on customer acquisition has led to a slow increase in occupancy for newly delivered or 1-2-year-old projects, significantly reducing the base of new customers. The pace of sales far exceeds the growth rate of occupancy demand, gradually shrinking the pool of potential buyers.  
2. Some customers from the second phase of Shanheda are not in a hurry to use their properties as they have not yet moved in, and they are waiting to make decisions after visiting the model area. At Zitai, the underground parking space prices are too high; the lower-priced parking spaces that customers are willing to accept have been gradually sold out, leaving limited options for customers.  
3. Slow integrated sales of cars and housing: The number of projects offering integrated car and housing sales has decreased, and the sales velocity of existing residential units remains low, resulting in a slower pace of integrated car and housing sales. There is still room for improvement in tapping into the needs of early owners.
